title: Four loops, four bugs
why.untouched: script.js is exactly as it was. The last loop is infinite — submit and see.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel below.
why.frozen: Your code never stops. It looped forever and was cut off.
hint2.frozen: Two hundred grams in scoops of fifteen. Check whether the number lands exactly on zero.
hint3.frozen: 200 minus 15 repeatedly gets to 5, then minus 10, then minus 25. The condition for exactly zero never comes true.
hint2.no-break: The coffee runs out at the eighth customer. There is no point walking the remaining twenty-two.
hint2.wrong-letters: Latte is five letters. If you get six, the loop runs one time too many.
hint3.wrong-letters: From zero, while you are BELOW the length.
hint2.wrong-checked: The stock is eight cups, and the loop walks thirty orders.
hint3.wrong-income: The equals sign replaces. Every turn wipes the previous one, and what survives is the price of one cup.
hint2.wrong-scoops: Two hundred grams in scoops of fifteen. Fourteen scoops, the last one incomplete.
hint3.wrong-scoops: Make the exit condition a range, not a point. Ask whether anything is left, not whether it ran out exactly.
why.wrong-count: There are not four lines in the console.
why.lost-p: A paragraph has disappeared from the page.
blocks.0.text: An end-of-day report. Four loops, each from a different lesson in this section.
blocks.1.text: One of them is infinite. Submit the code as it is and see what happens — submitting is protected and will tell you it froze.
blocks.2.text: What it is supposed to do
blocks.3.text: Product Latte, thirty orders, eight cups in stock, price four lev, two hundred grams of beans in scoops of fifteen.
blocks.4.items.0: the letters in the name are 5
blocks.4.items.1: only 8 customers are checked, because the coffee runs out
blocks.4.items.2: the income is 32 — eight cups at four
blocks.6.text: The infinite loop is the first bug to fix, wherever it sits. While it spins, nothing below it runs and you cannot check the rest.
blocks.7.text: Then come the quiet ones. The numbers appear, they look plausible, and they are wrong.
blocks.8.quote: Code that never finishes is easier to find than code that finishes with the wrong answer.
blocks.10.text: Fix the four loops. Do not touch the data.
blocks.11.text: Each bug comes from a lesson in this section. If you cannot place one, the hint ladder is under every failed check.
